source_of_truth: Completion is reached only when every feature has passes=true.

features.name: Establish a verified macOS compatibility baseline
acceptance: Document the current macOS runtime baseline for PRAW.
Record which workflows already pass on macOS and which ones are still Linux/bash-specific.
Turn the macOS adaptation work into explicit, reviewable follow-up tasks.
Local evidence shows tauri build reaches .app bundling on macOS and currently fails at the DMG packaging step.

features.name: Define an automated release lane for macOS artifacts
acceptance: Release automation has an explicit trigger contract such as tags or manual dispatch.
The workflow builds a signed-or-unsigned macOS artifact consistently in CI.
Release outputs and secrets/signing gaps are documented before rollout.
Phase 1 is allowed to ship .app bundles before DMG/signing/notarization is added.

features.name: Support zsh with the same dialog marker contract used by bash
acceptance: zsh shells are treated as dialog-capable by the front-end terminal state model.
The Rust shell integration layer injects OSC 133 prompt / command markers for zsh sessions.
Unsupported shells still fall back to classic mode instead of pretending to be dialog-safe.
Targeted zsh tests pass in both the TypeScript and Rust layers.
A runtime PTY test proves that an interactive zsh session emits real OSC 133 markers for executed commands.
